name: Abu al-Hutuf
title: Repentant of Karbala
shortDescription: Sa'd ibn al-Harith's brother who also broke away from the Kufan army upon hearing the cries of the Ahl al-Bayt's children and sacrificed his life for the Imam.
fullStory:
Abu al-Hutuf ibn al-Harith al-Ansari was the brother of Sa'd ibn al-Harith. Like his brother, he was from the Khawarij of Nahrawan who grew up with hatred for Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). On the day of Ashura, he stood with a drawn sword in Umar Sa'd's army to participate in shedding the blood of the Prophet's (PBUH) son.

As the hours of Ashura passed and the Imam's companions were martyred one by one, Abu al-Hutuf felt a wavering in his heart. The unparalleled bravery of the Imam's companions, their truth-seeking war poetry, and the innocence of the Hussaini caravan tickled his dormant conscience, but he still stood in the ranks of falsehood.

The moment of his transformation occurred in the final hours of the day with Imam Hussain's (AS) call for help. When the Imam called out: 'Is there any helper?' and the wailing and crying of women and children rose from the tents, Abu al-Hutuf's stony heart broke.

He, along with his brother Sa'd, turned the swords they had sharpened to fight the Imam against the army of falsehood. They shouted among Umar Sa'd's soldiers that they had returned to the truth and were defending the Prophet's son.

Abu al-Hutuf fought valiantly in the heart of the enemy army alongside his brother. Well-acquainted with combat techniques, they inflicted heavy damage on the enemy. After sustaining numerous injuries, both were martyred together in one spot, proving that it is never too late to repent and return to the embrace of Wilayah.
quoteArabic: أَلا ناصِرٌ يَنْصُرُني؟ أَلا ذابٌّ يَذُبُّ عَنْ حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ؟
quote: (Transformed upon hearing the Imam's call:) Is there any helper to help me? Is there any defender to defend the Prophet's sanctuary?
